title: Unicode in Uniface Applications
text: Unicode in Uniface Applications Unicode characters can be used in fields, variables, parameters, and the user interface, but there are some restrictions. In Uniface applications, Unicode characters can be used in: String data fields, variables, and parameters. String literals in the user interface, such as labels, menu items, and component titles. String parameters passed between the application and other components; for example, file I/O, or calling a WebService. Uniface provides the W packing code for string fields to contain Unicode characters, and a ProcScript function $string to generate Unicode characters from their codes. There are some restrictions in using Unicode characters: The first 128 Unicode characters (the ASCII characters) can be used everywhere in Uniface as long as the name conventions are satisfied; for example, control characters cannot be part of the object's names. The remaining Unicode characters: Are allowed in widgets, Unifields, initial values, and menu items. Are not allowed as object names, such as component names, entity names, and field names. Are allowed when passing string parameters to a non-Uniface component, if the component is Unicode-based such as Java. If you use non-ASCII characters in an assignment file, you need to save it in the Unicode (UTF-16 or UTF-8) encoding. For more information, see Restrictions on Use of Unicode and Installable Character Sets . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
